log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-packed PE32 executable with tags indicating persistence and runtime modules. I've already seen network activity showing C2 communication with bublikiadministrator.com. Now I need t

I have a good overview of the IOC types. The sample shows 1 unique IP, 3 domains, 12 registry persistence keys, 3 dropped files, and 4 processes created. The highlighted API calls (SetFileTime, SetWin

The network activity confirms C2 communication with bublikiadministrator.com (193.166.255.171) using HTTP GET requests with parameters that look like version/load indicators. The other domains (bublik

The dropped files reveal the malware's payload structure: cftmon.exe (dropped in multiple locations including AppData) and spools.exe (dropped in system32/drivers). These names are designed to blend i

The registry changes confirm a sophisticated persistence mechanism. The malware uses multiple techniques: 1) Run keys for both users (HKCU and HKLM) for cftmon.exe and spools.exe, 2) Winlogon Shell/Us

The process activity confirms the execution chain and reveals additional suspicious modules. The malware loads ftp33.dll (seen in dropped files and modules list) and mswsock.dll, wshtcpip.dll, hnetcfg
